texto: My love is like a red, red rose That's newly sprung in June: My love is like the melody That's sweetly played in tune. How fair art thou, my bonnie lass, So deep in love am I; And I will love thee still, my dear, Till all the seas gang dry. Till all the seas gang dry, my dear, And the rocks melt with the sun;I will love thee still, my dear, While the sands of life shall run. And fare thee weel, my only love. And fare thee weel awhile! And I will come again, my love, Though it were ten thousand mile.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Burns: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “My love is like a red, red rose That's newly sprung in June: My love is like the melody That's sw…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, poetry, still pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
